steps.title: Inventory real product decisions
actions: Cluster existing colors, type, spacing, geometry, components, states, and exceptions.
exit_criteria: Foundation and component priorities come from product evidence.

steps.title: Define direction and system principles
actions: Choose visual direction axes and signature signals.
Set semantic naming principles, density strategy, and accessibility commitments.
Record what the system intentionally will not standardize.
exit_criteria: Principles can resolve a new design decision without a screenshot reference.

steps.title: Build semantic foundations
actions: Create primitive scales, semantic roles, modes, and component aliases.
Test foreground/background and interactive state pairs.
Define typography, spacing, radius, motion, and asset conventions.
exit_criteria: Foundations support required modes and high-risk component states.

steps.title: Build primitives from contracts
actions: Prioritize high-frequency semantic controls and layout primitives.
Complete state, focus, content, and responsive contracts.
Use native or proven accessible behavior.
exit_criteria: Representative product flows can compose from stable primitives.

steps.title: Design migration and governance
actions: Publish adoption tiers, migration recipes, compatibility windows, and exception intake.
Automate deprecated usage detection where practical.
Define ownership, review cadence, and contribution expectations.
exit_criteria: Teams can adopt incrementally and legitimate gaps have a governed path.

steps.title: Validate in product, not only the library
actions: Migrate representative simple, dense, internationalized, and high-risk flows.
Measure visual coherence, accessibility, bundle cost, and exception frequency.
exit_criteria: Pilots pass gates and exceptions reveal no foundational design flaw.
